summary: ollama-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-side identity and credential-adjacent files — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and the GitHub CLI auth config at ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — and harvests the developer's git email, GitHub login and email, OS hostname, username, current working directory, platform, and CI provider. The collected JSON is then POSTed via https.request to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app, a generic Google Cloud Run URL unrelated to the package's advertised homepage. The behavior is labeled 'anonymous diagnostics' in the source but the payload contains directly identifying email addresses and host data. The package name and author identity ('Ollama JS Dev <hello@ollama-js.dev>', homepage ollama-js.dev) impersonate the official Ollama project, increasing install-base for the postinstall exfiltration. Reading ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — which also contains the GitHub OAuth token on real systems — at install time is well outside the package's stated Ollama-helper purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
